justifications.ip1: AC1 is tested by TestAC1OutputSchema with assertions on each required key and field type; AC2 by TestAC2ExitCode1 asserting returncode==1 with a fixture engineered for gap=0.20/0.30 > threshold 0.05; AC3 by TestAC3ExitCode0 asserting returncode==0 with threshold=1.0.
justifications.ip2: No out-of-scope items (CCCFA, Gini/ROC-AUC, retraining, database) appear anywhere; C1 is enforced by reading threshold from fairness-config.json with FAIRNESS_CONFIG_PATH env var as test-injection only; C2 is enforced with concrete stderr assertions.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files (scripts/evaluate_fairness.py, tests/test_evaluate_fairness.py, config/fairness-config.json) and provides complete code with concrete assertions like 'abs(eth_entry["gap"] - 0.75) < 1e-9'.
justifications.ip4: Task 2 writes the complete test file before the script exists with an explicit RED confirmation step ('Run pytest … before proceeding. All tests must fail') and Task 3 implements only after RED is confirmed.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 is tested in TestNFR1FMAFormula with exact numerical verification (expected_gap = 1.00 - 0.25 = 0.75) and a test distinguishing max-min from mean-deviation; NFR-2 tested by TestNFR2Determinism running two subprocess calls and asserting identical stdout; C1 threshold is never hardcoded.

notes: Exemplary plan: complete test code with engineered fixtures and numerical precision assertions, explicit RED confirmation, all constraints enforced at task level, and no out-of-scope scope creep.
